# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3' # Define the version of docker-compose file format (specifying a Docker Compose version) 
services:   # Start defining services here    
    elk_stack:         # Name your service with lowercase letters and no spaces, e.g., "logstash" for Logstash or Kibana in Elasticsearch stack         
        image: docker.elastic.co/elk/<your-dockerhub>:#tag (you can use this to tag the Docker Image)  # Set your preferred ELK Stack version here, e.g., elkstack_v5   
         ports:            # Expose services on specific port(s).     These need not be defined in every service if they are static  
             - "9201:9201" 
        volumes:           # Volumes define how the data should persist even when containers stop, start or restart. Here we map a directory from our host to an anonymous volume that only exists within Docker    
            - ./logs:/usr/share/elk-stack/data   : Set this location for logging your Elasticsearch and Kibana logs    # Make sure the path on both hosts matches with container's volumes.       This is just a sample, you can use any directory according to requirements 
        networks:           # Define what network(s) ELK Stack services are connected in     (optional but recommended for larger setups or if deploying across multiple machines).    A default one will be created as well  
            - elk_network             : Set your preferred name here, e.g., "elk-net"  Here you can use the networks with docker commands like `docker network ls` to list all existing Network names or run  `<command> --help for more details on a command>`    The default one will be used when not specified
        depends_on:         # Define services that this service should wait until it's started.     (optional)  e.g., elasticsearch, logstash   This is where you specify which other containers your Elastic Search and Log Stash are dependent on for initialization    elk-stack will only start if all these dependencies have been fully initialized
            - es                      : Set this service here according to requirement     The default value used when not specified. In case of any error, it might fail or simply skip starting the elasticsearch in logs   # Replace <service_name> with your actual ElasticSearch Service name  e.g., 'elastic'
            - ls                      : Set this service here according to requirement     The default value used when not specified    In case of any error, it might fail or simply skip starting the logstash in logs   # Replace <service_name> with your actual LogStash Service name e.g., 'logstash'
            - kib                      : Set this service here according to requirement     The default value used when not specified    In case of any error, it might fail or simply skip starting the Kibana in logs   # Replace <service_name> with your actual Elasticsearch Service name e.g., 'kib'
